senses.definition: someone who has been trained to care for injured or sick people, particularly in a hospital

examples.x: I appreciate the hard work and dedication of nurses in keeping us healthy and safe.
I thanked the nurse for her compassionate care during my stay in the hospital.
My sister is studying to become a nurse so she can take care of patients in the hospital.
Nurses play an important role in providing healthcare and assisting doctors in treating patients.
The nurse kindly explained the procedure to me and helped me feel at ease.

senses.definition: someone who is an expert in the field of food and nutrition
a branch of health care that is primarily concerned with promoting people's health and well being through occupation
a person whose job is to test people's eyes and sight or to make and supply glasses or contacts

examples.x: The optician recommended new glasses for better vision.
She visited the optician to get a prescription for her eyeglasses.
The optician adjusted the frames to fit his face perfectly.
After the eye exam, the optician explained the different lens options.
I made an appointment with the optician for a routine eye checkup.

senses.definition: a professional whose job is examining people's eyes and telling them what type of glasses they should wear

examples.x: The optometrist conducted a thorough eye examination to determine the patient's prescription.
She decided to visit the optometrist after experiencing blurred vision while reading.
The optometrist recommended a new pair of glasses to improve his vision.
The optometrist's clinic offers a range of services, including contact lens fittings and vision screenings.
As an optometrist, she specializes in diagnosing and treating eye conditions.

senses.definition: a hospital attendant whose job is to help medical staff with activities that do not require special medical training
a trained individual who provides emergency medical care to people before taking them to the hospital

examples.x: The paramedic arrived at the accident scene and immediately began assessing the injured.
She decided to pursue a career as a paramedic to help people in critical situations.
Paramedics play a crucial role in responding to medical emergencies and saving lives.
The ambulance crew includes paramedics who are trained to handle a wide range of medical emergencies.

senses.definition: a person who is licensed to diagnose and treat illness and disease under the supervision of a licensed physician
a person who provides assistance, often in a healthcare or service setting, such as a hospital or clinic

examples.x: The attendant helped the elderly patient with mobility issues during the hospital stay.
The attendant at the nursing home ensured that the residents received their medications on time.
The attendant in the ER guided patients to the appropriate rooms for treatment.
She worked as a nurse attendant, assisting doctors with patient care.

senses.definition: someone who looks after a child or an old, sick, or disabled person at home

examples.x: She quit her job to become a full-time caregiver for her elderly mother.
The daycare center employs skilled caregivers who provide nurturing care to young children.
The caregiver assisted the patient with daily tasks such as bathing and dressing.
Many caregivers experience burnout from the physical and emotional demands of their role.
The support group offers resources and advice for caregivers of individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

senses.definition: someone hired to oversee and care for a property, person, or group

examples.x: As a caretaker of the historic building, she ensures its preservation and upkeep.
The family hired a caretaker to help their loved one with mobility challenges.
A caretaker oversees the maintenance and landscaping of the community park.
The hired caretaker assists the elderly woman with daily tasks such as cooking and bathing.
As the caretaker of the playground, he ensures the equipment is safe for children to use.

senses.definition: a professional whose job is treating physical disorders concerned with movements of limbs by giving massages, exercises, etc.

examples.x: The physiotherapist helped the athlete recover from a knee injury through targeted exercises.
She decided to see a physiotherapist for her chronic back pain.
The physiotherapist specializes in treating patients with neurological conditions.
As a physiotherapist, he works closely with patients to improve their mobility and strength.
The physiotherapist recommended a personalized treatment plan to address the patient's muscle stiffness.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who assists pharmacists in dispensing medications, managing inventory, and processing prescriptions in a pharmacy setting
a healthcare professional who helps pharmacists with tasks like dispensing medications, managing inventory, and processing prescriptions in a pharmacy setting
a professional who helps individuals improve their communication and swallowing skills

examples.x: The speech therapist helps with issues like stuttering or trouble pronouncing words.
Children with speech difficulties often see a speech therapist for help.
If someone has trouble swallowing, a speech therapist can provide valuable assistance.
Speech therapists work with both children and adults to enhance their overall communication abilities.
After a stroke, a person might work with a speech therapist to regain language skills.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who specializes in helping individuals improve mobility, relieve pain, and restore function through exercises and physical interventions

examples.x: In hospitals, a physical therapist may assist patients recovering from surgeries or accidents.
The physical therapist uses hands-on techniques and exercises to treat muscle and joint issues.
If someone has difficulty walking, a physical therapist can provide exercises to improve mobility.
After a sports injury, athletes often work with a physical therapist for rehabilitation.
Physical therapists work with patients to improve balance and coordination after surgeries.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who evaluates, diagnoses, and treats patients with respiratory disorders using treatments, therapies, and ventilator management
a healthcare professional who performs diagnostic imaging procedures, such as X-rays, CT scans, MRI scans, and other imaging studies
someone who is in charge of all nursing services in a hospital
a nurse who is in charge of a hospital ward
a doctor who is highly trained in a particular area of medicine

examples.x: She consulted a dermatologist, a specialist in skin conditions, for her persistent rash.
The specialist provided detailed advice on managing the rare disease based on their extensive research.
He saw an orthopedic specialist to get a second opinion on his knee injury.
The specialist’s office is located in the city’s medical district.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who assists surgeons and other members of the surgical team during surgical procedures
a healthcare professional who performs electroencephalogram (EEG) tests to measure and record electrical activity in the brain
a healthcare professional who provides direct care to patients in a variety of healthcare settings
a healthcare professional who uses specialized equipment to perform ultrasound examinations and capture images of internal body structures for diagnostic purposes
a healthcare professional who provides pre-hospital emergency medical care
a person who is trained to treat a particular type of disease or disorder, particularly by using a specific therapy
a practitioner who specializes in using the medicinal properties of plants for promoting health and treating various health issues

examples.x: The herbalist uses plants to help people with health and wellness.
People interested in natural healing seek advice from an experienced herbalist.
Traditional medicine often includes the wisdom and practices of skilled herbalists.
A visit to the herbalist may involve discussing lifestyle and dietary changes.
The herbalist may teach about the benefits of different herbs for overall well-being.

senses.definition: a professional who studies and analyzes the patterns, causes, and effects of diseases within populations to improve public health

examples.x: During a pandemic, epidemiologists play a key role in managing and preventing the spread of illness.
Epidemiologists analyze data to identify patterns and causes of diseases.
If there's an outbreak, an epidemiologist investigates to understand and control it.
Governments rely on the insights of epidemiologists to implement effective health policies.
The epidemiologist studies how diseases spread and affect communities.

senses.definition: a nurse with advanced training who can diagnose, treat, and prescribe medicine

examples.x: The nurse practitioner is a highly-trained nurse who can do more than traditional nurses.
Some people prefer seeing a nurse practitioner for routine healthcare needs.
The flexibility and expertise of nurse practitioners contribute to accessible and quality healthcare.
Nurse practitioners focus on preventive care, helping patients stay healthy through education and screenings.
In rural areas or underserved communities, nurse practitioners play a crucial role in healthcare delivery.

senses.definition: a licensed healthcare professional who provides a variety of medical care to patients

examples.x: When you're sick, a registered nurse helps with your medical needs.
In emergencies, the registered nurse acts quickly to provide immediate care.
If you have questions about your health, the registered nurse is there to help.
Nursing homes and schools often have registered nurses to attend to health needs.
The compassion and expertise of registered nurses contribute to quality patient care.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who provides basic nursing care under supervision after completing specific education and passing a licensing examination

examples.x: The licensed practical nurse collaborates with the healthcare team to ensure quality patient care.
The compassionate care of a licensed practical nurse contributes to a positive patient experience.
Community health clinics often employ licensed practical nurses for primary care services.
The skills and dedication of licensed practical nurses enhance healthcare delivery in various settings.
In nursing homes, licensed practical nurses play a crucial role in resident care.

senses.definition: a healthcare professional who provides basic patient care under the supervision of a registered nurse or licensed practical nurse in healthcare settings
someone who uses hypnosis to induce a relaxed state or address specific conditions in individuals

examples.x: People might see a hypnotist to overcome fears or phobias.
The hypnotist tailors sessions to address specific needs or goals.
In some cases, a hypnotist might be part of a therapeutic approach for mental health.
Hypnotists use calming techniques to guide individuals into a trance.

senses.definition: a person who utilizes hypnosis to treat people with physical or emotional problems
a professional who helps patients and families navigate healthcare systems, offering emotional support and resources

examples.x: Hospital social workers offer a comforting presence during challenging times.
If you have questions about insurance, a hospital social worker can provide information.
Hospital social workers offer a comforting presence during challenging times.
After a difficult diagnosis, a hospital social worker may help with coping strategies.
The compassionate work of a hospital social worker contributes to improved patient well-being.

senses.definition: a professional who promotes and maintains cleanliness and health standards

examples.x: The hygienist ensured that all the medical equipment was sterilized before the surgery.
In the food industry, a hygienist regularly inspects kitchens to maintain high standards of cleanliness.
The hotel hired a hygienist to oversee the sanitation protocols in all guest rooms.
The school brought in a hygienist to teach the children about proper handwashing techniques and hygiene practices.

senses.definition: a trained professional who offers support to pregnant individuals and their families before, during, and after childbirth

examples.x: During labor, the doula provides comfort and encouragement.
Parents-to-be often hire a doula for personalized birthing assistance.
After childbirth, the doula continues to offer support and guidance.

senses.definition: a trained professional who utilizes essential oils and their therapeutic properties to promote physical and emotional well-being in clients
a person who is trained in acupuncture
a health care professional who treats and examines patients through direct contact
someone who gives professional advice on a given subject

examples.x: The financial consultant put forward a comprehensive investment plan for her clients, outlining various strategies to achieve their financial goals.
As a consultant in the field of marketing, she provided professional advice to businesses on how to enhance their brand visibility and reach their target audience effectively.
The IT consultant offered expert guidance to companies regarding their technological infrastructure, helping them streamline operations and maximize efficiency.
With her background in finance, Sarah became a sought-after consultant, assisting clients with investment strategies and financial planning tailored to their goals.
As a healthcare consultant, his role involved offering specialized advice to hospitals and medical institutions on improving patient care and optimizing operational workflows.

senses.definition: a person believed to be able to cure diseases or treat people using natural powers
someone who treats an ill person by giving them small doses of the substance that caused their illness
a health practitioner who treats illnesses using natural remedies, rather than artificial drugs
a licensed practitioner who uses talk therapy to help people with their thoughts and feelings

examples.x: The analyst helped her explore and understand her emotions during the counseling session.
She decided to see an analyst to work through her anxiety and gain coping strategies.
As an analyst, he provides a safe space for clients to talk about their thoughts and concerns.
The analyst uses various techniques to guide individuals in managing stress and improving mental well-being.
The analyst's role is to listen, support, and offer insights to promote positive changes in clients' lives.

senses.definition: a licensed professional who cleans teeth and provides preventive dental care to patients
a trained healthcare professional who assists dentists in providing oral healthcare services to patients
